title: Threads 1
description: Rust's standard library provides threads for concurrent execution. Use `thread::spawn` to create new threads.
instructions: Fix the code to properly spawn and join threads.
hints: thread::spawn returns a JoinHandle - save it to join later.
Call handle.join().unwrap() to wait for each thread to finish.
explanation: `thread::spawn` creates a new OS thread and returns a `JoinHandle`. The `move` keyword moves captured variables into the closure (important for thread safety). Calling `.join()` on the handle blocks until the thread finishes and returns a Result. Always join threads to ensure they complete before the main thread exits.
